channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran has been confiscating property from critics of the regime
item.description: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.content:encoded: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: The White House is planning a big prayer event. Almost all the speakers are Christian
item.description: As the White House prepares for a prayer event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary, some question why the lineup is almost entirely Christian.
item.content:encoded: As the White House prepares for a prayer event tied to the nation's 250th anniversary, some question why the lineup is almost entirely Christian.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: With plummeting support for immigration policies, Trump administration shifts approach
item.description: The Trump administration is shifting their public approach on immigration as Congressional Republicans push to fund ICE through the end of Trump's term.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration is shifting their public approach on immigration as Congressional Republicans push to fund ICE through the end of Trump's term.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Under Trump, State Department language on migration echoes that of white nationalists
item.description: The State Department is changing the way the U.S. talks about migration and refugees, embracing the "Great Replacement" theory promoted by white nationalists.
item.content:encoded: The State Department is changing the way the U.S. talks about migration and refugees, embracing the "Great Replacement" theory promoted by white nationalists.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: 'Pocket gardens' give native plants a place to thrive in cities
item.description: "Pocket gardens" of native plant species are becoming more common in urban areas. We tag along with a volunteer tending to tiny gardens in a neighborhood of Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded: "Pocket gardens" of native plant species are becoming more common in urban areas. We tag along with a volunteer tending to tiny gardens in a neighborhood of Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Julie Depenbrock

item.title: 'I'm OK with my scars': Hayden Panettiere on the struggles that shape her life
item.description: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with actress Hayden Panettiere about her new memoir, This is Me, and some of the challenges she's faced, from bullying as a child to losing custody of her own child.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with actress Hayden Panettiere about her new memoir, This is Me, and some of the challenges she's faced, from bullying as a child to losing custody of her own child.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer

item.title: Remembering Joe Sedelmaier, director of iconic 1970s and '80s TV ads
item.description: We remember Joe Sedelmaier, the man behind some of the most iconic TV ads of the 1970s and '80s. The commercials were not splashy, but their characters and catchphrases became part of popular culture.
item.content:encoded: We remember Joe Sedelmaier, the man behind some of the most iconic TV ads of the 1970s and '80s. The commercials were not splashy, but their characters and catchphrases became part of popular culture.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu

item.title: Actor and musician Rita Wilson talks about what she learned from her parents
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor and musician Rita Wilson talks about what she learned from her parents.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor and musician Rita Wilson talks about what she learned from her parents.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: How a nurse helped a mom through open heart surgery
item.description: Barb Barnes faced a very difficult moment in 2005, after she underwent major open heart surgery. A nurse helped her through it.
item.content:encoded: Barb Barnes faced a very difficult moment in 2005, after she underwent major open heart surgery. A nurse helped her through it.

item.title: What were the top takeaways from Trump's China visit?
item.description: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer speaks with Sarah Beran, former senior director for China and Taiwan Affairs at the White House National Security Council, about President Trump's recent trip to China.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer speaks with Sarah Beran, former senior director for China and Taiwan Affairs at the White House National Security Council, about President Trump's recent trip to China.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee
